title: The Evolution of Java GUI: From AWT to Swing
description: A timeline showing the progression of Java's GUI toolkits, highlighting the limitations of AWT that led to the creation of Swing.
relationships: AWT's heavy reliance on native peers led to a 'lowest common denominator' problem and platform-specific bugs
This limitation was the primary catalyst for developing a new, lightweight GUI framework
Swing was introduced as part of JFC to provide a more robust, feature-rich, and consistent cross-platform GUI solution
